claim: Consolidated fear memories when reactivated return to a labile state requiring de novo protein synthesis for reconsolidation
Protein synthesis inhibition in amygdala immediately after retrieval produces amnesia; 6h delay has no effect

dmPFC encodes uncertainty in belief evaluation supporting dual neural process model
BTSP enables one-shot content-addressable memory creation with binary synapses in hippocampal CA1
Goal setting intervention selectively reduces effort sensitivity; cognitive restructuring selectively reduces internal attribution of negative events
Psilocybin reduces confidence in negative self-beliefs; EEG entropy correlates with belief change and improved well-being
50 vs 25 sessions of schema therapy produces greater depression reduction (d=0.53) and higher remission (74% vs 58%)
No significant difference between DBT and schema therapy for BPD; both produce large improvements

Psilocybin reduces anterior hippocampus-DMN connectivity for 3+ weeks post-dose
Horizontal eye movements accelerate fear extinction learning in classical conditioning (18.2% vs 40.9% CR occurrence)
No difference between EMDR with eye movements and retrieval-only on neurocognitive outcomes

ImRs for PTSD: 70-86% remission; effect sizes d=1.40-2.66
Bayesian brain theory formalizes belief encoding and updating through precision-weighted prediction errors
MBIs induce neuroplastic changes in DMN, salience network, and central executive network
Neuroplasticity without therapeutic guidance may maladaptively consolidate; psychotherapy essential to direct change

Jungian psychotherapy: moderate-to-large effect sizes for symptom reduction (d=0.56-1.17)
